executive_summary: Cyber threat intelligence digest for 2026-03-09. 74 live items from 14 sources. 10 CRITICAL and 22 HIGH severity events in the last 36 hours. Top threats: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Qualcomm 0-Day, iOS Exploit Chains, AirSnitch Attack & Vibe-Coded Malware; Web Server Exploits and Mimikatz Used in Attacks Targeting Asian Critical Infrastructure; Chinese Cyber Threat Lurks In Critical Asian Sectors for Years. All items are from today only — no recycled or old news.
executive_summary_en: Cyber threat intelligence digest for 2026-03-09. 74 live items from 14 sources. 10 CRITICAL and 22 HIGH severity events in the last 36 hours. Top threats: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Qualcomm 0-Day, iOS Exploit Chains, AirSnitch Attack & Vibe-Coded Malware; Web Server Exploits and Mimikatz Used in Attacks Targeting Asian Critical Infrastructure; Chinese Cyber Threat Lurks In Critical Asian Sectors for Years. All items are from today only — no recycled or old news.

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Qualcomm 0-Day, iOS Exploit Chains, AirSnitch Attack & Vibe-Coded Malware
items.summary:
Another week in cybersecurity. Another week of "you've got to be kidding me."
Attackers were busy. Defenders were busy. And somewhere in the middle, a whole lot of people had a very bad Monday morning. That's kind of just how it goes now.
The good news? There were some actual wins this week. Real ones. The kind where the good guys showed up, did the work, and made a dent. It doesn't always

items.title: Web Server Exploits and Mimikatz Used in Attacks Targeting Asian Critical Infrastructure
items.summary:
High-value organizations located in South, Southeast, and East Asia have been targeted by a Chinese threat actor as part of a years-long campaign.
The activity, which has targeted aviation, energy, government, law enforcement, pharmaceutical, technology, and telecommunications sectors, has been attributed by Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 to a previously undocumented threat activity group dubbed

items.title: Chinese Cyber Threat Lurks In Critical Asian Sectors for Years
items.summary: An undefined Chinese-speaking actor wields a combo of custom malware, open source tools, and LOTL binaries against Windows and Linux, likely for spying.

items.title: Critical Nginx UI flaw CVE-2026-27944 exposes server backups
items.summary: Nginx UI flaw CVE-2026-27944 lets attackers download and decrypt server backups without authentication, exposing sensitive data on public management interfaces. A critical vulnerability in Nginx UI, tracked as CVE-2026-27944 (CVSS score of 9.8), allows attackers to download and decrypt full server backups without authentication. The flaw poses a serious risk to organizations exposing the managemen

items.title: Apache ZooKeeper Flaw Exposes Sensitive Data to Attackers
items.summary: Apache ZooKeeper, a centralized service used for maintaining configuration information and naming in distributed systems, has received critical security updates. The Apache Software Foundation recently addressed two “Important” severity vulnerabilities that could expose sensitive data and allow server impersonation in production environments. Configuration and Hostname Verification Fla

items.title: Cognizant’s TriZetto Provider Solutions data breach impacted over 3.4 million patients
items.summary: A breach at Cognizant’s TriZetto Provider Solutions exposed sensitive health data belonging to more than 3.4 million patients. A data breach at Cognizant’s TriZetto Provider Solutions exposed sensitive information belonging to more than 3.4 million patients. At this time, no ransomware group has claimed responsibility for the attack yet. TriZetto Provider Solutions is a healthcare […]

items.title: ClickFix Attack Uses Windows Terminal to Evade Detection
items.summary:
Fake CAPTCHA pages instruct victims to paste malicious commands in the Windows Terminal instead of the Run dialog.
The post ClickFix Attack Uses Windows Terminal to Evade Detection appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Target U.S. Critical Infrastructure Amid Rising Cyber Threats
items.summary: Iran-linked threat actors are escalating cyber operations against U.S. and allied networks, with Seedworm recently deploying new backdoors against critical infrastructure and high-value organizations amid the current regional conflict. Activity associated with the Iranian APT group Seedworm (aka MuddyWater, Temp Zagros, Static Kitten) has been observed on the networks of multiple U.S. organization

items.title: Cyber Espionage Group CL-UNK-1068 Linked to China Targets Asian Infrastructure
items.summary: A highly sophisticated cyber espionage group, designated as CL-UNK-1068, has been actively targeting critical infrastructure across South, Southeast, and East Asia since at least 2020. Originating from China, the threat actors focus on high-value sectors, including aviation, energy, government, law enforcement, technology, and telecommunications. The attackers use a versatile mix of custom malware

CVE-2026-3703 [CVSS 9.8] — A flaw has been found in Wavlink NU516U1 251208. This affects the function sub_4
items.summary: A flaw has been found in Wavlink NU516U1 251208. This affects the function sub_401A10 of the file /cgi-bin/login.cgi. Executing a manipulation of the argument ipaddr can lead to out-of-bounds write. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been published and may be used. Upgrading the affected component is recommended. The vendor was contacted early, responded in a very professiona

items.title: CVE-2026-3701 [CVSS 8.8] — A security vulnerability has been detected in H3C Magic B1 up to 100R004. Affect
items.summary: A security vulnerability has been detected in H3C Magic B1 up to 100R004. Affected by this vulnerability is the function Edit_BasicSSID_5G of the file /goform/aspForm. Such manipulation of the argument param leads to buffer overflow. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond

items.title: CVE-2026-3705 [CVSS 7.3] — A vulnerability was found in code-projects Simple Flight Ticket Booking System 1
items.summary: A vulnerability was found in code-projects Simple Flight Ticket Booking System 1.0. This issue affects some unknown processing of the file /Adminsearch.php. The manipulation of the argument flightno results in sql injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

items.title: CVE-2026-3708 [CVSS 7.3] — A security flaw has been discovered in code-projects Simple Flight Ticket Bookin
items.summary: A security flaw has been discovered in code-projects Simple Flight Ticket Booking System 1.0. The impacted element is an unknown function of the file /login.php. Performing a manipulation of the argument Username results in sql injection. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-3709 [CVSS 7.3] — A weakness has been identified in code-projects Simple Flight Ticket Booking Sys
items.summary: A weakness has been identified in code-projects Simple Flight Ticket Booking System 1.0. This affects an unknown function of the file /register.php. Executing a manipulation of the argument Username can lead to sql injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-3715 [CVSS 8.8] — A vulnerability was found in Wavlink WL-WN579X3-C 231124. This affects the funct
items.summary: A vulnerability was found in Wavlink WL-WN579X3-C 231124. This affects the function sub_40139C of the file /cgi-bin/firewall.cgi. Performing a manipulation of the argument del_flag results in stack-based buffer overflow. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. Upgrading to version 20260226 is able to mitigate this issue. You should upgrad

items.title: CVE-2026-3723 [CVSS 7.3] — A security flaw has been discovered in code-projects Simple Flight Ticket Bookin
items.summary: A security flaw has been discovered in code-projects Simple Flight Ticket Booking System 1.0. This affects an unknown function of the file /Admindelete.php. The manipulation of the argument flightno results in sql injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-3704 [CVSS 4.7] — A vulnerability has been found in Wavlink NU516U1 251208. This vulnerability aff
items.summary: A vulnerability has been found in Wavlink NU516U1 251208. This vulnerability affects the function sub_405B2C of the file /cgi-bin/firewall.cgi of the component Incomplete Fix CVE-2025-10959. The manipulation leads to command injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. It is recommended to upgrade the affected component. T

items.summary: Researchers uncovered fake Claude Code install pages spreading infostealers that steal passwords and browser sessions.

items.title: Quiz sites trick users into enabling unwanted browser notifications
items.summary: The quiz is just bait. The real goal is to win permission to send browser notifications that can later be used for ads, scams, or shady promotions.

items.title: Ring doorbells: Won’t you see my neighbor? (Lock and Code S07E05)
items.summary: This week on the Lock and Code podcast, we speak with Matthew Guariglia about Ring smart doorbells and the surveillance network they create.

items.title: FBI warns of phishing attacks impersonating US city, county officials
items.summary: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warns that criminals are impersonating U.S. officials in phishing attacks targeting businesses and individuals who request city and county planning and zoning permits. [...]

items.title: Why Password Audits Miss the Accounts Attackers Actually Want
items.summary: Password audits often focus on complexity rules but miss the accounts attackers actually target. Specops Software explains how breached passwords, orphaned users, and service accounts can leave organizations exposed. [...]

items.title: Can the Security Platform Finally Deliver for the Mid-Market?
items.summary:
Mid-market organizations are constantly striving to achieve security levels on a par with their enterprise peers. With heightened awareness of supply chain attacks, your customers and business partners are defining the security level you must meet.
What if you could be the enabler for your organization to remain competitive — and help win business — by easily demonstrating that you meet these

items.title: Chrome Extension Turns Malicious After Ownership Transfer, Enabling Code Injection and Data Theft
items.summary:
Two Google Chrome extensions have turned malicious after what appears to be a case of ownership transfer, offering attackers a way to push malware to downstream customers, inject arbitrary code, and harvest sensitive data.
The extensions in question, both originally associated with a developer named "akshayanuonline@gmail.com" (BuildMelon), are listed below -

QuickLens - Search Screen with

items.title: Internet Infrastructure TLD .arpa Abused in Phishing Attacks
items.summary:
Abusing DNS record management controls, the threat actor hides the location of malicious content via Cloudflare.
The post Internet Infrastructure TLD .arpa Abused in Phishing Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cloned AI Tool Sites Distribute Malware in ‘InstallFix’ Campaign
items.summary:
Threat actors replace legitimate commands on the cloned installation webpages with malicious commands.
The post Cloned AI Tool Sites Distribute Malware in ‘InstallFix’ Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Massive GitHub malware operation spreads BoryptGrab stealer
items.summary: Trend Micro found BoryptGrab stealer spreading through 100+ GitHub repositories, stealing browser data, crypto wallets, system information, and user files. Trend Micro uncovered a campaign distributing the BoryptGrab information stealer through more than 100 GitHub repositories. BoryptGrab is designed to collect browser and cryptocurrency wallet data, system details, and common files. Some variant

raw_items.summary: Threat actors are abusing the special-use ".arpa" domain and IPv6 reverse DNS in phishing campaigns that more easily evade domain reputation checks and email security gateways. [...]

raw_items.title: Fake CleanMyMac Site Spreads SHub Stealer, Targets Crypto Wallets
raw_items.summary:
Hackers are abusing a fake CleanMyMac download page to infect macOS users with SHub Stealer. This powerful infostealer drains crypto wallets and hijacks sensitive data. Instead of offering a standard installer, the page shows an “advanced” installation step telling users to “Open Terminal and paste the following command,” a pattern known in recent Mac campaigns as […]

raw_items.title: BoryptGrab Malware Abuses GitHub to Steal Browser and Crypto Wallet Data
raw_items.summary: A new Windows stealer dubbed BoryptGrab is being distributed through a large, ongoing campaign abusing fake GitHub repositories that pose as free tools, game cheats, and popular utilities. The malware focuses on stealing browser data, cryptocurrency wallet information, and system details, while also grabbing screenshots, common files, Telegram data, Discord tokens, and stored passwords. raw_items.title: ClipXDaemon Malware Targets Crypto Users in Linux X11 Sessions
raw_items.summary: ClipXDaemon is a new Linux malware family that hijacks cryptocurrency clipboard data in X11 sessions, operating fully offline without any command‑and‑control (C2) infrastructure. It reuses a ShadowHS-style loader built with the public bincrypter framework but delivers a completely different, autonomous financial payload.
